source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As shown in the figure, a uniform electric field is formed between two parallel metal plates $A$ and $B$ carrying opposite charges. An electron is injected into the electric field along the central line perpendicular to the direction of the electric field with an initial velocity $v_{0} = 4 \times 10^{6} \, \mathrm{m/s}$ from point $O$. When the electron exits the electric field at point $C$ on the right edge, its velocity direction forms a $30^{\circ}$ angle with the direction of $v_{0}$. The charge of the electron is $e = 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \, \mathrm{C}$, and the mass of the electron is $m = 0.91 \times 10^{-30} \, \mathrm{kg}$.

1. What is the kinetic energy of the electron at point $C$ in $\mathrm{J}$?
2. What is the magnitude of the potential difference between points $O$ and $C$ in $\mathrm{V}$?

issues: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths reference different image files.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
the setup uses named spatial elements from the diagram (parallel plates A/B, point O, point C, central line, electric-field direction)
multi_step: the problem has two linked subquestions: kinetic energy at C and potential difference between O and C
solving requires combining multiple givens: initial speed, exit direction angle 30°, electron charge, and electron mass
this is a physics derivation rather than direct lookup, involving at least intermediate reasoning from motion in a uniform electric field to energy/potential quantities
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with two explicit numeric answers and units
the requested outputs are standard quantitative physics values that can be recomputed from the stated givens
the final answers are constrained enough for rule-based or numeric tolerance checking

risk_flags: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths reference different image files.

decision_reasons: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths reference different image files.
